## Codex runtime adapter

- Use Codex collaboration tools (`spawn_agent`, `send_message`, `wait_agent`, `list_agents`, `interrupt_agent`) when they are available.
- Codex has no Claude Agent Team shared runtime. The parent agent owns wave state, checkpoints, aggregation, and conflict resolution.
- Keep `.claude/**` as the editable source of truth. Read generated `.agents/**` and `.codex/**` for runtime discovery, but never edit it directly.
- After changing `CLAUDE.md` or `.claude/**`, run `python .claude/shared/scripts/sync_to_codex.py --project . --apply --prune`.


# Command workflow: /start-session

Treat Claude-only command syntax as workflow intent and use the available Codex tools.

Start of Agent Team working session.

Use for complex multi-day projects.

Steps:
1. If work/backlog.md exists, read it — incomplete tasks from the previous session; otherwise skip
2. Read .claude/agent-memory/team-lead/MEMORY.md — improvement context (see "Historical IMPROVEMENT_LOG" section)
3. Read the latest report from work/session-reports/
4. Ask the user what to work on today
5. Begin coordination (follow instructions from team-lead.md):
   - Complex task → spawn_agent(task_name="architect") for decomposition
   - Simple task → spawn_agent(task_name="coder") directly
   - For features → /interview (creates user-spec.md)

Env: CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 (already in settings.json)
